creator: freebsd@g3os.org
text:
OVERVIEW:
When I run startx, the (X) server can't load the vesa driver and without a working driver, X fails to start.  The log indicates that ioBase can't be found in vesa_drv.so but I can see it defined in vesa.h which vesa.c includes.


STEPS TO REPRODUCE:

1) Tell xorg to use the vesa driver (the radeon driver doesn't seem to be available for PowerPC)
$ cat /usr/local/etc/X11/xorg.conf.d/driver-radeon.conf
# https://www.freebsd.org/doc/handbook/x-config.html#x-config-video-cards-file
# http://mac.linux.be/content/g4-emac-1ghz-radeon

Section "Device"
	Identifier "ATI Technologies Inc Radeon RV200 QW [Radeon 7500]"
	# Driver     "radeon"
	Driver     "vesa"
	BusID      "PCI:0:16:0"
EndSection

2) As a user (i.e. not root), run `startx`

Note I've tried building xorg from ports as well as using prebuilt packages from https://joshcummings.net/pub/FreeBSD/FreeBSD%3a10%3apowerpc/2016Q1/All/ which were apparently built using Poudriere.


ACTUAL RESULTS: 
(stdout)
$ startx
xauth:  file /home/damonz/.serverauth.887 does not exist
xauth: (argv):1:  bad display name "emac.local:0" in "list" command
xauth: (stdin):1:  bad display name "emac.local:0" in "add" command

EXPECTED RESULTS: xorg starts and displays twm and xterm


BUILD DATE & HARDWARE: 

I have an Apple eMac.

$ uname -a
FreeBSD emac.local 10.3-RELEASE FreeBSD 10.3-RELEASE #0 r297264: Fri Mar 25 05:18:20 UTC 2016     root@releng1.nyi.freebsd.org:/usr/obj/powerpc.powerpc/usr/src/sys/GENERIC  powerpc

    cap 02[80] = AGP 4x 2x 1x SBA disabled

    device     = 'RV200 [Radeon 7500/7500 LE]'

    subclass   = VGA
    cap 02[58] = AGP 4x 2x 1x SBA disabled
    cap 01[50] = powerspec 2  supports D0 D1 D2 D3  current D0

cpulist0: <Open Firmware CPU Group> on ofwbus0
cpu0: <Open Firmware CPU> on cpulist0


ADDITIONAL NOTES:
In spite of the ports manual I don't know enough about patching ports yet to change the code and print things out.  When I try to make changes to /usr/ports/x11-drivers/xf86-video-vesa/work/xf86-video-vesa-2.3.4/src/vesa.c, make install clobbers them.

Let me know if I can provide other details.

Thank you,
- d
